case_title: DILLINGHAM & MURPHY, LLP VS. RAILROAD TECHNOLOGY CORPORATION , A CORPORATION et al

docket_entries.description: NOTICE TO PLAINTIFF

POS OF OPPOSITION TO DEFTS' MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS FOR LACK OF PERSONAL JURISDICTION, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, MOTION TO STAY OF DISMISS ACTION ON BASIS OF INCONVENIENT FORUM; DECLARATION OF THOMAS J KLITGAARD; REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE FILED BY PLAINTIFF DILLINGHAM & MURPHY, LLP

LAW AND MOTION 302, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT RAILROAD TECHNOLOGY CORPORATION, ROBERT NESS' MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF HAS PRODUCED FACTS SHOWING DEFENDANTS NESS AND RAILROAD TECHNOLOGY CORPORATION HAVE CALIFORNIA CONTACTS RELEVANT TO THIS CASE. AMONG OTHER FACTS, PLAINTIFF HAS SHOWN THAT THE RETAINER AGREEMENT WAS ENTERED INTO IN CALIFORNIA; SERVICES ON BEHALF OF DEFENDANTS WERE PERFORMED IN CALIFORNIA; MR. NESS WAS A RESIDENT IN CALIFORNIA WHEN THE CAUSE OF ACTION AROSE; AND NESS AND RTC CONDUCTED BUSINESS IN CALIFORNIA. DEFENDANTS HAVE NOT MET THEIR BURDEN OF STAYING OR DISMISSING THIS CASE FOR FORUM NON CONVENIENS. BALANCING THE PUBLIC AND PRIVATE FACTORS, CALIFORNIA HAS A FAR GREATER INTEREST IN HEARING THIS MATTER THAN FLORIDA. SEE STANGVIK V. SHILEY INC. (1991) 54 CAL. APP. 3D 744. MOST OF THE EVENTS TOOK PLACE IN CALIFORNIA; NO EVENTS TOOK PLACE IN FLORIDA. PLAINTIFF'S RECORDS AND TIMEKEEPERS ARE IN CALIFORNIA. THE CASE INVOLVES A DISPUTE RELATING TO THE BAR AND RECOVERY OF ATTORNEY'S FEES; FLORIDA HAS NO INTEREST IN SUCH A LAWSUIT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN #6185.

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Dec-13-2013 Vacated

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-09-2013 Set For Trial On Jan-13-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
Off Calendar Apr-17-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: THOMAS J. KLITGAARD, ESQ., 415.397.2700, FOR PLAINTIFF DILLINGHAM & MURPHY, LLP; No other appearances.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT RAILROAD TECHNOLOGY CORPORATION, ROBERT NESS' MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS is denied. Plaintiff has produced facts showing Defendants Ness and Railroad Technology Corporation have California contacts relevant to this case. Among other facts, Plaintiff has shown that the retainer agreement was entered into in California; services on behalf of Defendants were performed in California; Mr. Ness was a resident in California when the cause of action arose; and Ness and RTC conducted business in California. Defendants have not met their burden of staying or dismissing this case for forum non conveniens. Balancing the public and private factors, California has a far greater interest in hearing this matter than Florida. See Stangvik v. Shiley Inc. (1991) 54 Cal. App. 3d 744. Most of the events took place in California; no events took place in Florida. Plaintiff's records and timekeepers are in California. The case involves a dispute relating to the Bar and recovery of attorney's fees; Florida has no interest in such a lawsuit. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Anthony Vaughn #6185.

MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
Off Calendar Mar-28-2013 Continued To Apr-17-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS RAILROAD TECHNOLOGY CORPORATION , A CORPORATION & ROBERT NESS' MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS ACTION ON BASIS OF INCONVENIENT FORUM is continued on the Court's own motion to April 17, 2013. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer #5976.
